goal: Replace production Playwright browser launching and login navigation with user-owned manual Chrome startup plus verified existing-tab URL binding over loopback CDP.
non_goals: Do not copy or inspect credentials, cookies, passwords, local storage, or the user's normal Chrome profile.
Do not bypass CAPTCHA, human verification, provider anti-bot checks, or browser security controls.
Do not treat a pasted URL as authentication without finding and validating the same existing tab in the connected manual browser.
Do not replace fake-provider launch-mode E2E tests or add official model APIs in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Production runtime never launches Chrome or navigates provider/login pages automatically.
A user-started dedicated Chrome exposes a loopback-only CDP endpoint and keeps login and verification entirely manual.

Provider URL binding validates the allowlisted origin, strips query/hash from returned or persisted data, finds an existing matching tab, rejects login/verification states, and requires a usable composer.
Bound provider turns reuse the exact page without creating or navigating tabs.
Login or verification loss pauses the provider and requires explicit URL revalidation before retry.
The control page exposes per-provider URL fields and clear unbound, verified, and blocked states on desktop and mobile.
Launch-mode fake-provider E2E, server API tests, manual browser launcher tests, and the full local runtime suite pass.
